archetype_reasoning: Despite trading 86 distinct markets with apparent diversity, the open positions reveal a dominant pattern of near-certainty NO trades at 0.97-0.999 price levels (e.g., US declares war on Iran at 0.998, Israel strikes Iran at 0.994, Putin meets Zelenskyy at 0.992). The directionality of 0.97 confirms nearly all trades go the same direction. Recent resolved trades show multiple losses precisely on near-certainty positions (Israel-Hamas ceasefire NO at 0.954, 0.95), exposing the classic yield-harvesting blowup risk where rare events wipe accumulated gains.

domain_summary: Best genuine edge appears in 'Other' (85.7% accuracy) and Iran_ME (73.6%), though both may be inflated by near-certainty position harvesting. Europe and Russia_UKR accuracy suggests limited genuine forecasting edge outside obvious outcomes.
signal_weight_reasoning: The extreme directionality (0.97) combined with near-certainty open positions (7+ positions at 0.97+ price) indicates this trader is primarily harvesting yield on foregone conclusions rather than providing genuine forecasting signal. Recent string of 15 consecutive losses in resolved trades also suggests a deteriorating edge or a concentrated bad-period in contested markets. Genuine directional calls in the 0.10-0.80 range are rare.

Near-certainty buys on volatile geopolitical events (ceasefire NO at 0.95) — classic yield-harvester vulnerability

open_positions_summary: Portfolio is dominated by near-certainty NO positions across Iran escalation (US war on Iran, Israel strikes Iran, Iranian regime collapse), Russia-Ukraine (Putin-Zelenskyy meeting), and European politics (Denmark PM candidates, AFD in government). The implied thesis is 'extreme geopolitical events won't happen in the near term.' Two anomalous positions — Fordow YES at 0.965 and Mette Frederiksen YES at 0.93 — contradict the dominant strategy and may represent hedges or errors. Keiko Fujimori YES at 0.66 is the sole genuinely contested directional bet.

summary: This trader presents as a yield harvester masquerading as a broad geopolitical forecaster. While the 86-market breadth and positive realized PnL suggest sophistication, the 0.97 directionality, clustering of open positions at 0.97-0.999, and a string of 15 consecutive recent losses all point to a trader systematically collecting small premiums on 'obvious' outcomes rather than genuine forecasting. The strategy is structurally vulnerable to tail events, as demonstrated by losing near-certainty ceasefire NO positions. Genuine signal exists only in isolated cases (Fujimori, Nasralla) at contested price levels; do not follow this trader's near-certainty positions as forecasting intelligence.
